_meta.purpose: Canonical tag vocabulary for the CoThesis Compendium ingestion/enrichment pipeline. One uniform node shape across all taxonomies. Resolve free-text resource descriptions to tag codes; tag at the FINEST confident level; rollup is up-only (handled at query time, do NOT also tag parents).

tagging_rules.rollup: up-only: a leaf tag satisfies ancestor filters; an ancestor tag does NOT satisfy descendant filters
tagging_rules.tag_at: finest level you are confident about; stop there (do not add parent tags)
tagging_rules.multi_tag: a resource may carry multiple tags within and across taxonomies

resource_type.when_to_use: Tag at SUBTYPE when the resource's format is specific (e.g. 'systematic review article' → article/systematic_review_article); tag at TYPE when only the broad format is clear (e.g. 'a journal article' → article).

specialty.when_to_use: Tag at SPECIALTY when the resource targets a named specialty; tag at GROUPING when it spans a whole grouping. synonyms carry international/regional name variations (US/UK/India spellings + abbreviations) — match against these so e.g. "anesthesiology" (US) hits ANAES and "pediatrics" hits PAEDS.
specialty.note: Specialty set = canonical in-scope subset (70) of the 75-code master. Domain 7 Biomedical/bench-science specialties (BIOCHEM, MICROBIO, etc.) excluded per health-research scope lock, consistent with PRE methodology removal. synonyms sourced from cothesis_specialty_dataset_spec v1.1 regional_names.

nodes.name: Medical Genetics
nodes._flag: canonical collision: spec categorised GENETICS as Biomedical Sciences (bench); reassigned to clinical Medical Genetics per Domain 1 intent. Confirm in canonical graph.

nodes.name: Dermatology
nodes._preserved: RACP/ACD specialty dropped by canonical-71; retained for trainee coverage

nodes._preserved: RACP/ACD specialty dropped by canonical-71; retained for trainee coverage

methodology.note: paradigm is present on each category for future use but is NOT an MVP tag level.
methodology.when_to_use: Tag at METHODOLOGY when the resource is about a specific method (e.g. 'how to do a cohort study' → the cohort methodology); tag at CATEGORY when it's study-type-general (e.g. 'intro to observational research' → OBS). child_terms and synonyms include regional name variations and subtypes — match against these, not just the title.

cross_specialty_domain.when_to_use: Single level. Tag when a resource addresses a cross-cutting domain (e.g. health economics, patient safety) regardless of specialty.

foundation_skill.when_to_use: Single level. Tag when a resource teaches a transferable research skill (e.g. critical appraisal, data management) used across methods.

thesis.when_to_use: Tag at STAGE when a resource helps with a specific stage task (e.g. sample size → EV-03); tag at PHASE when broad (e.g. a whole "how to do a systematic review" guide → spans HI/EV/SH phases). UNLIKE other taxonomies, match on DELIVERABLES/BLOCKERS and the seo search_terms — NOT the canonical name (trainees search "sample size", not "Sample/Selection Strategy").
thesis.note: name = canonical framework label (stable, do not rename); match on search_terms + deliverables + tag_here, NOT name. Each stage now carries: search_terms (how trainees search), deliverables (what the stage produces — primary tag signal), tag_here (resource types that belong here), and disambiguate (overlap-zone rules). Tag finest stage on deliverable/blocker match; phase-level only when genuinely phase-broad. The method↔stage joins: method→design EV-01, analysis IN-02 (synthesis→HI-04), reporting SH-01.

nodes.name: Topic Identification & Scoping
search_terms: how to find a research question
nodes.description: Identify research area of interest, assess scope, and explore initial feasibility based on available resources and context
nodes.deliverables: topic statement, scope definition, feasibility notes.
nodes.tag_here: topic-generation aids, "how to find a research question" guides, scoping-the-field resources, gap-analysis tools, idea banks.
nodes.disambiguate: vs HI — TH-01 is *pre-literature* exploratory scoping; once systematically searching, it's HI-01. Vs TH-02 — TH-01 is the broad area, TH-02 the precise question.

nodes.name: Question/Aim Formulation
nodes.description: Develop focused research question(s) or study aims using appropriate frameworks (PICO, SPIDER, PEO, etc.)
nodes.tag_here: PICO/PECO/SPIDER frameworks, FINER criteria, hypothesis-writing guides, aim-vs-objective explainers, question-type→design mapping. **The single most-requested early resource** — high tagging priority.

nodes.name: Theoretical Framing
nodes.description: Establish theoretical basis for the study: hypotheses for quantitative work, conceptual frameworks, guiding theories, or methodological positioning for qualitative work
nodes.tag_here: theory-selection guides, conceptual-framework templates, paradigm/epistemology primers. **Links to the paradigm tier** — a "positivist vs interpretivist" resource tags TH-03 and carries `paradigm` relevance.

nodes.name: Feasibility Assessment
nodes.description: Systematic assessment of resources, timeline, data access, expertise, and overall project viability
nodes.tag_here: feasibility checklists, Gantt/timeline templates, "is this doable in training time" guides, data-access scoping. **Resolves blocker:** "topic too broad/narrow."

nodes.name: Search Strategy Development
nodes.description: Define search approach including databases, search terms, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and documentation method
nodes.deliverables: search strategy, database list, search terms.
nodes.tag_here: database guides (PubMed/Embase/CINAHL), MeSH/Emtree builders, search-string syntax, PRISMA-S, librarian-consultation.
nodes.disambiguate: HI-01 is the *preliminary landscape* search; EV produces the *finalised, reproducible* search for a review's Methods. Same skill, different rigour. Broad search-skill resources → **both**; PRISMA-S/protocol-grade → EV.

nodes.name: Evidence Gathering
nodes.description: Execute search strategy, screen results, and select relevant evidence or literature for inclusion
nodes.tag_here: screening tools (Rayyan, Covidence), title/abstract screening guides, reference managers (Zotero/EndNote), de-duplication.

nodes.name: Quality Assessment
nodes.description: Appraise quality of evidence, assess risk of bias, and evaluate strength of included sources
nodes.tag_here: appraisal tools (CASP, JBI, ROBIS, RoB 2, Newcastle-Ottawa, AMSTAR-2, GRADE), bias primers. **Resolves blocker:** "can't judge study quality." **Links to FoundationSkill** Critical Appraisal.

nodes.name: Synthesis & Positioning
nodes.description: Synthesise findings from gathered evidence, identify gaps, and position current study within existing knowledge
nodes.tag_here: narrative-synthesis guides, evidence-table templates, "writing a lit review", gap-statement guides.
nodes.disambiguate: HI-04 synthesises *others'* evidence; IN interprets *your own* results. A thematic-synthesis resource for a *review* → HI-04; for *primary* qual data → IN-02.

nodes.name: Study Design Specification
nodes.description: Define methodology, design type, and core study parameters
nodes.tag_here: design-selection guides, design-comparison resources, **the methodology entries themselves** — every methodology in the taxonomy is most relevant at EV-01. This is the natural join between the method taxonomy and the stage taxonomy.

nodes.name: Construct Operationalization
nodes.description: Define and operationalize variables, outcome measures, exposures, or constructs to be studied
nodes.tag_here: operationalisation guides, data-dictionary templates, variable-typing, endpoint selection (primary/secondary). **Links to FoundationSkill** Measurement; **MEAS** methods relevant.

nodes.name: Sample/Selection Strategy
nodes.description: Determine sampling approach, sample size, selection criteria, and recruitment or selection procedures
nodes.deliverables: sampling strategy, power calculation, recruitment plan.
nodes.tag_here: sample-size calculators, power-analysis guides (G*Power), sampling-method explainers (probability/purposive/theoretical), recruitment. **Resolves blocker:** "don't know my sample size" — high demand.

nodes.name: Data Acquisition Planning
nodes.description: Plan data collection, extraction, or acquisition procedures including instruments, protocols, and quality measures
nodes.tag_here: protocol templates, CRF/questionnaire design, interview-guide construction, SOP templates.

nodes.name: Governance & Registration
nodes.description: Ethics application, protocol registration, and obtaining necessary institutional approvals
nodes.tag_here: HREC/IRB guides, consent/PICF templates, trial/PROSPERO registration, governance/site-approval, data-management-plan + privacy. **Resolves blocker:** "ethics is a black box" — very high demand. **Links to compliance overlays** and `BIOETHICS` domain.

nodes.name: Pilot & Calibration
nodes.description: Test procedures, calibrate instruments, train team members, and refine protocols before full execution
nodes.tag_here: pilot-study guides, inter-rater reliability (kappa), instrument-refinement, feasibility-pilot resources.

nodes.name: Data Acquisition Execution
nodes.description: Execute data collection, extraction, or acquisition according to protocol
nodes.tag_here: collection tools (REDCap, Qualtrics), fieldwork guides, interview/observation conduct, retention strategies.

nodes.name: Quality Assurance
nodes.description: Monitor data quality, verify accuracy, resolve discrepancies, and ensure protocol adherence
nodes.tag_here: data-monitoring, missing-data handling, source-data verification, ongoing-reliability resources.

nodes.name: Data Management
nodes.description: Clean, organise, secure, and prepare data for analysis
nodes.tag_here: data-cleaning guides, codebook templates, secure-storage/governance, version-control, de-identification. **Links to FoundationSkill** Data Management.

nodes.name: Analysis Preparation
nodes.description: Prepare data for analysis, check assumptions, and finalise analysis plan
nodes.tag_here: statistical-assumption guides (normality etc.), data-transformation, SAP templates, analysis pre-registration. **Resolves blocker:** "which test do I use" — pair with IN-02.

nodes.name: Primary Analysis
nodes.description: Execute main analysis, synthesis, or interpretation according to plan
nodes.tag_here: statistical-test guides, software tutorials (R/SPSS/Stata/Python), qualitative-coding (NVivo, thematic/framework analysis), test-selection flowcharts. **Highest-volume resource demand in the framework** — discriminate by method (stats vs qual-coding vs synthesis). **Links to** the analytical method's taxonomy entry.

nodes.name: Results Interpretation
nodes.description: Interpret findings in context, assess clinical/practical significance, and relate to existing evidence
nodes.tag_here: effect-size/clinical-significance guides, "interpreting your results", results-vs-literature framing. **Disambiguate vs SH-01:** IN-03 is *understanding* what results mean; SH-01 is *writing them up*.
nodes.disambiguate: IN-03 is *understanding* what results mean; SH-01 is *writing them up*.

nodes.name: Supplementary Analyses
nodes.description: Conduct sensitivity analyses, subgroup analyses, and robustness checks
nodes.tag_here: sensitivity-analysis guides, subgroup-analysis cautions, multiple-comparisons corrections, robustness resources.

nodes.name: Output Drafting
nodes.description: Draft manuscript, report, or recommendations based on study findings
nodes.tag_here: reporting guidelines (**STROBE, PRISMA, CONSORT, COREQ, SRQR, CARE, SQUIRE** — tag each to SH-01 *and* to its method via `reporting_standards`), IMRaD-structure guides, academic-writing, section-by-section drafting. **Links heavily to** the methodology `reporting_standards` field.

nodes.name: Refinement & Review
nodes.description: Revise draft based on self-review, co-author feedback, and supervisor input
nodes.tag_here: revision guides, responding-to-reviewers, co-author/supervisor feedback workflows, editing/proofing. **Loop note:** SH-02→SH-01 is the canonical revision loop.

nodes.name: Dissemination Planning
nodes.description: Select dissemination venue, plan implementation of findings, and prepare submission
nodes.tag_here: journal-selection tools, conference-abstract guides, poster/oral-presentation design, target-formatting (college vs journal), authorship/ICMJE. **Links to** presentation resources.

nodes.name: Completion & Follow-up
nodes.description: Submit output, respond to reviewers, conduct re-audit if applicable, and ensure sustainment
nodes.tag_here: submission-system guides, peer-review-process explainers, RANZCP/college-submission specifics, publication/preprint, post-publication (altmetrics). **Loop note:** SH-04→SH-01 = RANZCP resubmission loop. **Resolves blocker:** "what happens after I submit."
